2604.04470.title: MC-GenRef: Annotation-free mammography microcalcification segmentation with generative posterior refinement
2604.04470.paper_summary: Microcalcification (MC) analysis is clinically important in screening mammography because clustered puncta can be an early sign of malignancy, yet dense MC segmentation remains challenging: targets are extremely small and sparse, dense pixel-level labels are expensive and ambiguous, and cross-site shift often induces texture-driven false positives and missed puncta in dense tissue. We propose MC-GenRef, a real dense-label-free framework that combines high-fidelity synthetic supervision with test-time generative posterior refinement (TT-GPR). During training, real negative mammogram patches are used as backgrounds, and physically plausible MC patterns are injected through a lightweight image formation model with local contrast modulation and blur, yielding exact image-mask pairs without real dense annotation. Using only these synthetic labeled pairs, MC-GenRef trains a base segmentor and a seed-conditioned rectified-flow (RF) generator that serves as a controllable generative prior. During inference, TT-GPR treats segmentation as approximate posterior inference: it derives a sparse seed from the current prediction, forms seed-consistent RF projections, converts them into case-specific surrogate targets through the frozen segmentor, and iteratively refines the logits with overlap-consistent and edge-aware regularization. On INbreast, the synthetic-only initializer achieved the best Dice without real dense annotations, while TT-GPR improved miss-sensitive performance to Recall and FNR, with strong class-balanced behavior (Bal.Acc., G-Mean). On an external private Yonsei cohort ( n=50 ), TT-GPR consistently improved the synthetic-only initializer under cross-site shift, increasing Dice and Recall while reducing FNR. These results suggest that test-time generative posterior refinement is a practical route to reduce MC misses and improve robustness without additional real dense labeling.
2604.04470.paper_summary_zh: 微鈣化 (MC) 分析在篩檢乳房攝影中具有臨床重要性，因為聚集的點狀物可能是惡性腫瘤的早期徵兆，但密集的 MC 分割仍然具有挑戰性：目標極小且稀疏，密集的像素級標籤昂貴且模糊，跨站點偏移常常導致基於紋理的假陽性和在密集組織中漏掉的點狀物。我們提出了 MC-GenRef，一種真正的無密集標籤框架，結合了高保真度的合成監督和測試時生成後驗精煉 (TT-GPR)。在訓練過程中，真實的負面乳房 X 光片區塊用作背景，並通過一種輕量級的影像形成模型注入物理上合理的 MC 圖案，該模型具有局部對比度調節和模糊效果，從而產生精確的影像-標記對，而不需要真實的密集標註。僅使用這些合成標註對，MC-GenRef 訓練了一個基本分割器和一個種子條件的校正流 (RF) 生成器，該生成器作為可控的生成先驗。在推理過程中，TT-GPR 將分割視為近似後驗推理：它從當前預測中導出一個稀疏種子，形成種子一致的 RF 投影，通過凍結的分割器將其轉換為特定案例的替代目標，並通過重疊一致性和邊緣感知正則化迭代精煉 logits。在 INbreast 上，僅使用合成的初始化器在沒有真實密集標註的情況下達到了最佳的 Dice，而 TT-GPR 改善了對漏檢敏感的性能，提升了召回率和假陰性率 (FNR)，並展現出強大的類別平衡行為 (Bal.Acc., G-Mean)。在一個外部私有的延世隊列 (n=50) 中，TT-GPR 在跨站點偏移下持續改善了僅使用合成的初始化器，增加了 Dice 和召回率，同時降低了 FNR。這些結果表明，測試時生成後驗精煉是一種實用的途徑，可以減少 MC 漏檢並在不需要額外的真實密集標註下提高穩健性。
2604.04470.author: Hyunwoo Cho et.al.
2604.04470.authors: Hyunwoo Cho, Yeeun Kwon, Min Jung Kim, Yangmo Yoo
